SEARCH
Package com.boschrexroth.api

Interface Bulk

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable
public interface Bulk extends AutoCloseable

creates a ctrlX Data Layer bulk

This interface extends AutoCloseable, indicating that resources held by implementations of this interface should be properly released when no longer needed, typically by calling the close() method.

Basis for all examples 

 try (Bulk b = client.createBulk()) {
   ...
   ...
 }
 ...

  • Method Details

    • read

      Result read(BulkReadArg[] args)
      Read read values using a bulk operation. This function is synchronous. 
      
   ...
  ArrayList<BulkReadArg> list = new ArrayList<>();
  BulkReadArg arg = BulkReadArg.create(addr, null);
  list.add(arg);
  ...
  arg = BulkReadArg.create(addr_x, null);
  list.add(arg);
  ...
  Result res = b.read(list.toArray(BulkReadArg[]::new));
   ...
      Parameters:
      args - BulkReadArg a list of read arguments
      Returns:
      Result status of function call

    • read

      Result read(BulkResponseCallback callback, BulkReadArg[] args)
      Read read values using a bulk operation. This function is asynchronous.
      Parameters:
      callback - BulkResponseCallback is the callback of response (callback == null -> synchronous)
      args - BulkReadArg a list of read arguments
      Returns:
      Result status of function call

  ...

      Result delete(String[] addresses)
      Delete deletes nodes using a bulk operation. This function is synchronous. 
      
  ...
  Result res = b.delete(new String[]{ addr1, addr2 });
   ...

      Result delete(BulkResponseCallback callback, String[] addresses)
      Delete deletes nodes using a bulk operation. This function is asynchronous.

    • write

      Result write(BulkWriteArg[] args)
      Write writes nodes using a bulk operation. This function is synchronous. 
      
   ...
  ArrayList<BulkWriteArg> list = new ArrayList<>();
  Variant v = Variant.create();
  v.setBool8(true);
  BulkWriteArg arg = BulkWriteArg.create(addr, v);
  list.add(arg);
  ...
  v.setInt32(-1);
  arg = BulkWriteArg.create(addr_x, v);
  list.add(arg);
  ...
  Result res = b.create(list.toArray(BulkWriteArg[]::new));
   ...
      Parameters:
      args - BulkWriteArg is a list of write arguments
      Returns:
      Result status of function call
